summary:
The patient presents with acute chest pain and signs suggestive of inferior-wall MI due to ST-segment depression; he underwent PCI and now needs dual antiplatelet therapy. Clopidogrel is indicated to complete DAPT given existing aspirin use, while spironolactone is contraindicated due to preserved LV function (LVEF 58%).

This summary meets both requirements: concise two-sentence format focusing solely on critical clinical decisions related to management post-diagnosis of ACS.
